step_description: AWR wait event delta between snapshot brackets
narrative_hint: Log file sync accounts for 62.4% of wait time — 48,420 waits averaging 306ms each. This is extremely high and indicates a commit storm: many small transactions each waiting for redo to flush to disk. The near-identical count of log file sync (48,420) and log file parallel write (48,380) confirms each commit is waiting for its own individual redo write. This is classic batch-commit-storm behavior — a process committing after every row rather than batching commits. Claude should identify this as the root cause and ask for awr_sql_delta to identify which SQL and application module is responsible.
